channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: League champs! Oakland Tech boys capture outright OAL title with a dominant victory over Oakland
item.description: OAL boys basketball: Oakland Tech never trails against Oakland as league winning streak improves to 31.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: The Midwest golf company that's changing the game in Sycamore, Illinois
item.description: Sub 70, a golf company based in Sycamore, Illinois, offers custom-made golf equipment and personal service to its customers, while maintaining its Midwest roots and high-quality craftsmanship.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Bregman and Red Sox agree to $120 million, 3-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Alex Bregman and the Boston Red Sox have agreed to a $120 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the deal tells The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nick Pivetta agrees to $55 million, 4-year contract with San Diego Padres, AP source says
item.description: Nick Pivetta became the last of the high-profile free agent pitchers to reach an agreement this offseason, a backloaded $55 million, four-year contract with the San Diego Padres, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mets finalize Pete Alonso deal and re-sign injured reliever Drew Smith
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pete Alonso and the New York Mets finalized their $54 million, two-year contract on Wednesday as spring training opened in Florida with the team's first formal workout for pitchers and catchers.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California families celebrate Trump administration's probe into state's refusal to follow trans athlete order
item.description: Women and parents in California are celebrating the DOE's intention to investigate the state for refusing to comply with Trump's executive order on transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL hit with lawsuit from fans as teams are 'not allowed to' join Bluesky: reports
item.description: Two Bluesky users have reportedly sued the National Football League for keeping its teams off the app and forcing fans to use other social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baseball welcomes another season, with most pitchers and catchers reporting Wednesday
item.description: There's a new No. 2 in the New York Yankees' rotation behind Gerrit Cole. A familiar face is getting a fresh start leading the Cincinnati Reds. Meanwhile, Shohei Ohtani & Co. are preparing a title defense.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Education Department launches Title IX probes into 2 blue states allegedly ignoring trans athlete order
item.description: The Department of Education has launched a Title IX investigation into two athletic associations allegedly ignoring Trump and allowing trans athletes compete against girls and women.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Angels' Anthony Rendon out long term because of upcoming hip surgery
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Angels third baseman Anthony Rendon needs hip surgery and will be out for an extended period, his latest major injury setback since joining Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington man faces assault charges after allegedly attacking teen referees during son's hockey game
item.description: A Washington state man is accused of assaulting two teenage referees during his son's youth hockey game in an "unprovoked" attack, according to law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former coach Vilda denies pressure on Hermoso to downplay kiss after Women's World Cup final
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Jorge Vilda, the former coach of Spain women’s soccer team, told a judge on Wednesday that ex-soccer federation president Luis Rubiales did not ask him to put pressure on Jenni Hermoso following the outcry that erupted after Rubiales kissed the player.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pistons set franchise record with 42-point halftime lead in 132-92 romp over Bulls
item.description: Malik Beasley made seven 3-pointers and finished with 24 points, and the Detroit Pistons built a franchise-record, 42-point halftime lead on the way to a 132-92 romp over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Madsen scores 28, but Utes lose at Cincinnati
item.description: CINCINNATI (ABC4 Sports) - Despite 28 points from Gabe Madsen against his former team, the Runnin' Utes fell at Cincinnati Tuesday night, 85-75. Madsen, who began his college career with the Bearcats, made eight three-pointers on 22 attempts, in matching a season high point total.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Egor Demin leads BYU past West Virginia, 73-69
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.V. (ABC4 Sports) - Freshman Egor Demin scored 16 points to lead BYU to a 73-69 win over West Virginia on Tuesday night. Kanon Catchings added 11 points for BYU (16-8, 7-6 Big 12). "Gutsy win by our guys," said BYU head coach Kevin Young.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Boxing mogul in talks to stage fight at Alcatraz in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Five years ago, no one would have thought YouTuber Jake Paul would get in the ring last fall with Mike Tyson -- who is a boxing legend but has been retired for nearly 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Fox 5

item.title: US skier Breezy Johnson marks return from 14-month ban with a gold in world championship downhill
item.description: Her top speed of 86 mph (138.51 kph) was among the fastest ever recorded by a woman in an Alpine skiing race.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox agree to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago White Sox have agreed to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury that includes an invitation to big league spring training camp. The team announced the move on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blackhawks visit the Blues after Donato's 2-goal performance
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (17-31-6, in the Central Division) vs. St. Louis Blues (24-26-5, in the Central Division) St. Louis; Saturday, 7 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Blues -250, Blackhawks +203; over/under is 5.5 BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks visit the St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ryan Donato stars as the Blackhawks beat the slumping Predators 6-2
item.description: Ryan Donato had two goals and two assists, and the Chicago Blackhawks handed Nashville its sixth straight loss with a 6-2 victory over the Predators on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: McDavid makes perfect pass in overtime as the Oilers beat the Blackhawks 4-3
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor McDavid set up Zach Hyman in overtime, and the Edmonton Oilers beat the Chicago Blackhawks 4-3 on Wednesday night. Hyman redirected McDavid's pass for the power-play goal at 1:36. It was Hyman's 19th goal and McDavid's 49th assist of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
